hero.headline: MAHE Online MBA in Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #3 University 2025 | Institution of Eminence | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹2,92,000

sections.tldr: MAHE Online MBA in Operations Management covers ERP, TQM, supply chain management, project management, services operations, and operations research. NAAC A++ NIRF #3 University with IoE tag. Total fee ₹2,92,000 (₹73,000/sem). Zero-cost EMI ₹12,167/month. Coursera Plus bundled free. 8 scholarship categories. Work experience required; fresh graduates submit SOP video.

about.body:
MAHE's Online MBA in Operations Management covers both manufacturing and services operations with quantitative methods. Semester 3 covers enterprise resource planning (SAP/ERP modules, MRP, production planning integration), total quality management (Six Sigma basics, ISO 9001, Kaizen, statistical process control), and supply chain management (demand forecasting, inventory optimisation, vendor management, bullwhip effect). Semester 4 adds project management (CPM/PERT, Agile basics, risk management, earned value analysis), services operations management (service blueprint design, capacity planning for services, queue management), and operations research (linear programming, transportation and assignment models, simulation basics).

The operations research subject in Sem 4 is a differentiator. Most competing operations MBAs skip the quantitative modelling depth, but MAHE includes LP, transportation models, and simulation. This suits professionals in manufacturing, logistics, or consulting who need analytical rigour.

MAHE holds NIRF #3 University 2025, NAAC A++, and Institution of Eminence status. Coursera Plus is bundled free. At ₹2,92,000, the fee is premium but the NIRF #3 credential and quantitative operations depth justify evaluation for manufacturing and consulting careers.

Work experience in reputed organisations is required. Fresh graduates can apply with an SOP video.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Operations Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, and management decision-making using accounting data.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Contract law, consumer protection, intellectual property, company law basics, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Applied operations research project supervised by faculty.

subjects.name: Enterprise Resource Planning
subjects.desc: SAP/ERP module overview, MRP (Material Requirements Planning), production planning integration, inventory management in ERP, and ERP implementation lifecycle.

subjects.name: Total Quality Management
subjects.desc: Six Sigma basics (DMAIC), ISO 9001 quality management systems, Kaizen methodology, statistical process control (SPC), and quality cost analysis.

subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Demand forecasting methods, inventory optimisation (EOQ, safety stock), vendor management and evaluation, bullwhip effect, and supply chain network design.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business model canvas, lean startup methodology, and funding strategies.

subjects.name: Sustainability
subjects.desc: Sustainable business models, ESG frameworks, and CSR strategy.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Operations capstone integrating Sem 1-4 learning with an industry-linked process improvement problem.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: CPM/PERT network analysis, Agile and Scrum basics, risk management frameworks, earned value analysis, and project scheduling with resource constraints.

subjects.name: Services Operations Management
subjects.desc: Service blueprint design, capacity planning for service industries, queue management theory, service quality measurement (SERVQUAL), and service recovery strategies.

subjects.name: Operations Research
subjects.desc: Linear programming (graphical and simplex methods), transportation and assignment models, simulation basics, decision analysis, and game theory introduction.

whoHires.body:
MAHE MBA Operations Management graduates target roles across manufacturing, logistics, consulting, and services:

**Industries:** Manufacturing (auto, FMCG, pharma, electronics), logistics, e-commerce operations, consulting, IT services delivery, and infrastructure

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Supply Chain Analyst, Production Planning Associate, Quality Analyst, Project Coordinator, Process Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Production Manager, Quality Manager, Project Manager (PMP track), Process Excellence Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** COO, VP Operations, Head of Supply Chain, Director of Manufacturing, Chief Quality Officer, Head of Project Management Office

**Employers:** Manufacturing (Tata Motors, Maruti Suzuki, L&T, Godrej), FMCG (HUL, ITC, P&G), logistics (Delhivery, Blue Dart, DHL), consulting (McKinsey Operations, Deloitte Strategy & Operations), and IT services (TCS, Infosys delivery management).

**Note:** MAHE provides career support services. The Manipal alumni network is strong in manufacturing, healthcare operations, and IT services sectors.

skills.body:
The Operations Management specialisation builds skills across process optimisation, quality, and project management:

**Technical skills:**
- ERP: SAP module navigation, MRP calculation, production planning integration, and ERP implementation methodology
- Quality Management: Six Sigma DMAIC application, SPC chart construction, ISO 9001 audit preparation, and quality cost calculation
- Supply Chain: demand forecasting (moving average, exponential smoothing), EOQ/safety stock calculation, and vendor scorecard design
- Operations Research: LP formulation and solution, transportation model optimisation, and simulation model design

**Applied skills:**
- Project Management: CPM/PERT network construction, Agile sprint planning, earned value metrics, and risk register development
- Services Operations: service blueprint mapping, capacity utilisation calculation, and queue analysis

**Soft skills:** Managing cross-functional production teams, negotiating with vendors, presenting process improvement ROI to leadership, and leading quality culture change.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MAHE Online MBA Operations Management against peer programmes:

**vs MUJ Online MBA Operations Management:** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 on the same platform. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹1,12,000 more for the NIRF #3 and IoE brand. Both share similar LMS delivery. MAHE includes operations research as a standalone subject; MUJ may not. The content overlap is significant. Unless the MAHE flagship brand specifically matters, MUJ delivers strong value.

**vs Amity Online MBA Operations Management:** Amity at ₹2,25,000 with NIRF #22 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹67,000 more but carries NIRF #3. Amity has a larger alumni network. MAHE includes Coursera Plus.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations & Data Sciences:** NMIMS at approximately ₹1,96,000 combines operations with data sciences. MAHE's programme is purely operations-focused with OR quantitative depth. NMIMS has strong Mumbai manufacturing and consulting recruiter connections.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA Operations:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 is substantially more expensive but carries NIRF #3 and IoE. DPU includes edX; MAHE includes Coursera Plus.

fees.body:
The MAHE Online MBA in Operations Management has a total programme fee of ₹2,92,000 inclusive of all taxes.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹73,000
- Semester 2: ₹73,000
- Semester 3: ₹73,000
- Semester 4: ₹73,000
- **Total: ₹2,92,000**

**Application fee:** ₹500 one-time.

**Zero-cost EMI:** ₹12,167/month directly through the university.

**Scholarships (8 categories):**
- Defence Personnel / Ex-servicemen: 20%
- Divyaang / PwD: 20%
- Merit: 10% (75%+ aggregate)
- MAHE/AGE Alumni: 15%
- SAARC and EWC Nationals: 40%
- Female Learners: 15%
- Doctors: 15%
- Corporate Employees: 15%

**Important:** Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers.

**Bundled:** Coursera Plus access free with the programme.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with MAHE each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Operations Management as the specialisation, issued by Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #3 University rank, Institution of Eminence status, and WES recognition. Valid for manufacturing management promotions, PSU operations roles, MNC supply chain positions, and private-sector operations leadership. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from MAHE Online MBA Operations Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Suresh N.
items.liked: Operations Research as a full subject with LP and simulation. TQM covered Six Sigma DMAIC in practical depth
items.disliked: ₹2,92,000 is steep when MUJ offers operations management on the same platform for ₹1,80,000. The content overlap between the two is significant
items.body: I work in auto manufacturing and chose MAHE for the NIRF #3 credential. The Operations Research subject was the standout, covering LP formulation and simulation in depth that most online MBAs skip. TQM and Supply Chain Management were well-structured. The honest question is whether ₹1,12,000 extra over MUJ is worth it purely for the brand. For manufacturing employers who filter on university rank, yes. For everyone else, consider MUJ seriously.

items.name: Meera J.
items.liked: Project Management covered CPM/PERT and Agile, bridging traditional and modern approaches. ERP module gave SAP context
items.disliked: Services Operations Management felt lighter than the manufacturing-focused subjects. Career support could be more structured
items.body: Coming from IT services delivery, the Project Management and Services Operations subjects were directly applicable. The ERP module gave useful SAP context for enterprise clients. Coursera Plus supplemented with PMP prep content. NIRF #3 and IoE cleared credential filters at two consulting firms. The 15% corporate scholarship brought my costs down, which helped justify the premium over MUJ.

items.name: Amit G.
items.liked: Supply Chain Management covered demand forecasting and inventory optimisation with quantitative methods. Coursera Plus access was a genuine bonus
items.disliked: LMS video quality varied across subjects. Some operations lectures used outdated case examples. The loan-scholarship restriction was frustrating
items.body: The operations curriculum covers breadth well. Supply Chain, TQM, ERP, and Project Management give a solid operations foundation. My issue was the scholarship policy. I qualified for the merit scholarship (10%) but needed a loan for the rest. MAHE's policy disqualifies scholarships if you take financing, so I paid full price. At ₹2,92,000 without any discount, the value proposition weakens against DPU at ₹1,89,400 or MUJ at ₹1,80,000.

faqs.question: Does the Operations MBA include quantitative methods like Operations Research?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sem 4 includes Operations Research as a full subject covering linear programming, transportation and assignment models, simulation, and decision analysis. This quantitative depth is uncommon in competing online operations MBAs.

faqs.question: How does MAHE Operations compare to MUJ Operations?
faqs.answer: Same platform, similar delivery. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 carries NIRF #3 and IoE. MUJ at ₹1,80,000 costs ₹1,12,000 less. Choose MAHE if the flagship brand specifically matters for manufacturing or consulting employers. Choose MUJ if fee value is the priority.

faqs.question: Is ERP covered in the curriculum?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sem 3 includes Enterprise Resource Planning covering SAP/ERP module overview, MRP, production planning integration, and ERP implementation lifecycle.

faqs.question: Can loan applicants get scholarships?
faqs.answer: No. Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers. This is a critical consideration when comparing effective costs across programmes.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹2,92,000 total (₹73,000 per semester). ₹500 application fee. Zero-cost EMI at ₹12,167/month. 8 scholarship categories available (up to 40%). Reconfirm with our counsellor before payment.

chooseIf: NIRF #3 and Institution of Eminence credential matters for manufacturing or consulting employers.
You want Operations Research quantitative depth (LP, simulation) alongside practical operations subjects.
Coursera Plus access for PMP prep and supply chain certifications adds value.
You qualify for a scholarship category that reduces the ₹2,92,000 effective fee.
consider: MUJ at ₹1,80,000 offers similar platform delivery for ₹1,12,000 less.
NMIMS at ₹1,96,000 combines operations with data sciences and has Mumbai recruiter networks.
Loan financing disqualifies all scholarships, making MAHE one of the most expensive online MBA options.
